hoses: 5-inch LDH supply line from hydrant outlet to Engine 181 intake
200-foot 1¾-inch attack line from Engine 181 pump panel discharge to front door

problems.title: Total Flow Requirement
problems.question: Engine 181 has one ChiefXD 185 attack line flowing and a second identical ChiefXD 185 attack line is placed in service. Each line flows 185 GPM. What is the new total flow requirement?
problems.instructorExplanation: Total flow is the sum of all flowing attack lines.
problems.explainMistake: Do not answer for only one line once the second identical line is placed in service.

problems.title: Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying one 200 ft 1.75-inch attack line flowing 185 GPM through a ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle at 50 PSI. Use C = 15.5, no elevation, and no appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure is required?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Round FL to nearest 5 PSI: 105 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: The attack line friction loss rounds to 105 PSI. Adding the 50 PSI nozzle pressure gives a required pump discharge pressure of 155 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: A common mistake is stopping at friction loss only. Pump discharge pressure must include nozzle pressure after friction loss is calculated.

problems.title: Attack Line Friction Loss
problems.question: The attack line is 200 ft of 1.75-inch hose flowing 185 GPM. Using C = 15.5, how much friction loss is present in the attack line?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: Using the 1¾-inch hose coefficient and the listed flow, the attack line friction loss is 106.1 PSI, rounded to 105 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add nozzle pressure when the question asks only for friction loss. Friction loss is calculated before nozzle pressure is added.

problems.title: Nozzle Pressure
problems.question: The ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle is operating at its rated nozzle pressure. What nozzle pressure is required at the nozzle?
problems.instructorExplanation: The scenario information panel identifies the nozzle package. The ChiefXD 185 operates at 50 PSI nozzle pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Do not confuse pump discharge pressure with nozzle pressure. Nozzle pressure is only the pressure required at the nozzle.

problems.title: Hydrant Pressure Drop
problems.question: The hydrant static pressure is 70 PSI and residual pressure is 60 PSI while supplying one 185 GPM attack line. Using the FirePumpSim hydrant-drop rule, how many additional equivalent 185 GPM attack lines could be supplied?
formulaBreakdown: Pressure drop = static pressure − residual pressure.
Percent drop = pressure drop ÷ static pressure × 100.
Driver Operator hydrant rule: 10–15% drop supports 2 additional equivalent lines.
problems.instructorExplanation: The pressure drop is 14.3% of static pressure. Under the standard hydrant pressure-drop rule, that indicates the hydrant can supply two additional equivalent attack lines.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the 10 PSI drop as a 10% drop. The percentage must be based on static pressure: 10 divided by 70 equals 14.3%.

problems.title: Second Identical Attack Line
problems.question: If a second identical ChiefXD 185 attack line is deployed, the first line flows 185 GPM and the second line flows 185 GPM. What is the new total flow requirement?
formulaBreakdown: New total flow = flow per attack line × number of identical attack lines.
problems.instructorExplanation: A second identical attack line doubles the flow demand. Two 185 GPM lines require a total of 370 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not double the pump pressure to find total flow. Flow is added by line; pressure is calculated separately.

problems.title: Extended Attack Line Pump Pressure
problems.question: The original attack line is 200 ft of 1.75-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI. If the line is extended by two additional 50 ft sections, the total length becomes 300 ft. Use C = 15.5, no elevation, and no appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure is required?
formulaBreakdown: Extended hose length = 200 ft + 100 ft = 300 ft.
Round FL to nearest 5 PSI: 160 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: Adding 100 ft increases the friction loss enough to require a new pump setting of about 210 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the original 200 ft pump setting after adding hose. Recalculate friction loss using the full 300 ft length.

problems.title: Second-Floor Attack Line Pump Pressure
problems.question: The same 200 ft 1.75-inch ChiefXD 185 attack line is moved to a second-floor fire area. The nozzle flows 185 GPM at 50 PSI. Use C = 15.5 and add 5 PSI elevation for one floor above the pump. No appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure is required?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
problems.instructorExplanation: Moving the same line to the second floor adds 5 PSI for elevation. The hose friction loss and nozzle pressure stay the same.
problems.explainMistake: Do not leave the pump pressure at the ground-level setting. Add the elevation pressure for the second-floor operation.
